ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
title: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U)
shortDescription: Housing for All initiative providing central assistance to Urban Local Bodies and credit-linked subsidies for home loans.
eligibility: Urban residents without a pucca house in their name anywhere in India, meeting specified income criteria (EWS/LIG/MIG).

fees.details: No application fee on the official portal.

stepsToApply: Visit the official PMAY-U MIS portal and select 'Citizen Assessment' from the top menu.
Choose 'Apply Online' and select the appropriate component (e.g., Slum Dwellers, Beneficiary Led Construction, or Credit Linked Subsidy).
Enter your 12-digit Aadhaar number and your name exactly as it appears on the Aadhaar card to authenticate.
Fill out the comprehensive application form with your personal, income, residential, and bank account details.
Save the application, note down the generated assessment ID for future tracking, and download the printed form to submit to the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) or bank.

title: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
shortDescription: Financial assistance program to provide pucca houses with basic amenities to all houseless rural families.
eligibility: Houseless rural families or those living in kutcha/dilapidated houses, identified via the SECC 2011 data or Awaas+ survey.

fees.details: Grant-based scheme; no application fee.

stepsToApply: Beneficiaries are primarily selected based on SECC 2011/Awaas+ data. Visit the PMAY-G portal to check if your name is on the Permanent Wait List (PWL).
If listed, approach your local Gram Panchayat or Ward Member with your Aadhaar and bank details for physical verification.
The Gram Rozgar Sahayak will register your details on the AwaasSoft portal and geotag your proposed construction site.
Once sanctioned by the Block Development Office, the first installment is credited directly to your bank account via DBT.
Upload stage-wise construction photos using the AwaasApp to trigger subsequent financial installments.

ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
title: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
shortDescription: National scheme providing up to ₹78,000 subsidy to households for installing rooftop solar plants to get free electricity.
eligibility: Any Indian household with a valid electricity connection and a roof suitable for solar panel installation.

fees.details: Portal registration is free. Installation costs vary by vendor, offset by direct central subsidies.

stepsToApply: Visit the PM Surya Ghar portal or download the mobile app and click on 'Register'.
Select your State and Electricity Distribution Company (DISCOM), then enter your Electricity Consumer Number, mobile number, and email.
Log in with your Consumer Number and apply for rooftop solar by filling in your roof area and capacity requirements.
Wait for DISCOM feasibility approval. Once approved, select a registered vendor from the portal's directory to install the plant.
After installation, submit the plant details online, apply for net-metering, and upon successful DISCOM inspection, upload a canceled cheque to receive the subsidy via DBT.

ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
title: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
shortDescription: Provides free LPG (cooking gas) connections to women of Below Poverty Line (BPL) families to promote clean cooking fuel.
eligibility: Adult women belonging to BPL households, SC/ST, PMAY-G beneficiaries, or Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) with no existing LPG connection.

fees.details: Security deposit for cylinder and pressure regulator is paid by the Government.

requiredDocuments: Aadhaar Card (of applicant and adult family members)
stepsToApply: Visit the PMUY official portal and click on 'Apply for New Ujjwala 2.0 Connection'.
Choose your preferred Oil Marketing Company (Indane, BharatGas, or HP Gas) and click on their respective portal link.
Select 'Ujjwala Beneficiary Connection', enter your location details to find your nearest distributor, and verify your Aadhaar via e-KYC.
Fill out the comprehensive online application detailing your family composition and upload the required Ration Card and address proofs.
Alternatively, download the offline form and submit it physically at the nearest LPG distributorship; once verified, you will be called to collect your stove and first free cylinder.

title: Bharat Bill Payment System (BBPS)
shortDescription: An integrated, interoperable bill payment system for seamless payment of electricity, water, gas, DTH, and telecom bills.
eligibility: Any citizen with a bank account, UPI app, or visiting a CSC/Retail outlet.

fees.details: Zero convenience fee for most utility bill payments via UPI/Netbanking.

requiredDocuments: Consumer Number / Account ID from the respective utility bill
stepsToApply: Open your preferred UPI application (BHIM, PhonePe, Google Pay), banking app, or visit the BBPS web portal.
Navigate to the 'Recharge & Pay Bills' section and select the category (e.g., Electricity, Water, Piped Gas).
Select your state and search for your specific service provider/DISCOM from the BBPS directory.
Enter your unique Consumer Number or Account ID to instantly fetch your live bill amount.
Verify the bill details and complete the payment using your UPI PIN or banking credentials to receive an instant, assured BBPS confirmation receipt.

title: Sanchar Saathi - CEIR (Block Lost Phone)
shortDescription: Central Equipment Identity Register portal to block lost or stolen mobile phones across all telecom networks in India.
eligibility: Any individual whose mobile phone is lost or stolen in India.

fees.details: Completely free service.

stepsToApply: File a report for your lost/stolen phone with the local police station or through the state's online FIR portal.
Get a duplicate SIM card for the lost mobile number from your telecom operator to receive OTPs.
Visit the Sanchar Saathi portal, navigate to the CEIR section, and click on 'Block Stolen/Lost Mobile'.
Fill out the request form with the device's IMEI number, brand, model, and upload the FIR copy and purchase invoice.
Provide your new/duplicate mobile number for OTP verification, submit the request, and save the Request ID. The phone will be blacklisted across all Indian networks within 24 hours.

title: Sanchar Saathi - TAFCOP
shortDescription: Portal to check the number of mobile connections active against your name and report unauthorized numbers.
eligibility: Any Indian mobile subscriber holding an Aadhaar card.

fees.details: Free to check and report.

stepsToApply: Visit the Sanchar Saathi portal and click on the 'Know Your Mobile Connections (TAFCOP)' module.
Enter your current active mobile number, fill in the captcha code, and request an OTP.
Enter the received OTP to log into your personalized TAFCOP dashboard.
Review the list of all mobile numbers currently registered under your name/ID proof.
If you spot unrecognized numbers, select them, choose the option 'Not my number', and click 'Report' to initiate disconnection by the telecom provider.

department: Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation
title: Swachh Bharat Mission (IHHL Toilet Construction)
shortDescription: Financial assistance of ₹12,000 to eligible rural and urban households for the construction of Individual Household Latrines (IHHL).
eligibility: BPL households, and specific APL households (SC/ST, physically handicapped, women-headed) without an existing toilet.

fees.details: No application fees. Approved beneficiaries receive ₹12,000 as a grant.

stepsToApply: Visit the Swachh Bharat Mission (Gramin/Urban) portal and click on 'Application Form for IHHL'.
Register yourself by providing your mobile number, name, and address to generate a Login ID.
Log in and fill out the detailed application form, providing your Aadhaar details, exact geographic location, and bank account information.
Upload the scanned copy of your bank passbook and a clear passport-size photograph, then submit the form.
Your local nodal officer will conduct a physical inspection of your house. Upon approval, the funds will be released in two installments (pre and post-construction) via DBT.

department: Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation
title: Jal Jeevan Mission (Har Ghar Jal)
shortDescription: Initiative to provide a functional household tap connection (FHTC) providing safe drinking water to every rural household.
eligibility: All rural households in India currently without a piped drinking water connection.

fees.details: Installation is highly subsidized by the government; nominal community contribution or monthly tariffs may apply based on Gram Panchayat rules.

requiredDocuments: Property Ownership Document or Tax Receipt
stepsToApply: The scheme is community-driven. Approach your local Village Water and Sanitation Committee (VWSC) or Gram Panchayat office.
Submit a formal application requesting a Functional Household Tap Connection (FHTC) along with your identity and address proofs.
The Gram Panchayat includes your household in the Village Action Plan (VAP).
Once the village water infrastructure is laid by the Public Health Engineering Department (PHED), contractors will extend the pipeline to your premises.
A water meter (in applicable states) and tap will be installed, and you may need to sign a community maintenance agreement.

title: SVAMITVA Property Cards
shortDescription: Drone-based mapping of rural inhabited lands to provide a 'Record of Rights' (Property Card) to village household owners.
eligibility: Residents of rural 'Abadi' (inhabited) areas holding property but lacking formal documented ownership rights.

fees.details: Drone mapping and initial property card issuance are free.

stepsToApply: Pay attention to the notification issued by your District Magistrate regarding the scheduled date for drone flying in your village.
Prior to the drone survey, participate in the 'Chuna Lagana' (limestone marking) process with Patwari officials to accurately mark your property boundaries.
The Survey of India will conduct the drone mapping and generate a high-resolution map of the village.
A draft property map will be published at the Gram Panchayat. Check your property boundaries and file an objection within the stipulated 15-30 days if there is a discrepancy.
Once disputes are resolved, the final 'Property Card' (Sanad) is generated digitally and distributed by the Revenue Department, which can be downloaded via DigiLocker.

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
title: e-Dharti Geo Portal
shortDescription: Online portal for property mutation, conversion from leasehold to freehold, and substitution for L&DO administered properties.
eligibility: Allottees/owners of properties administered by the Land and Development Office (primarily in Delhi).

fees.details: Conversion and mutation charges vary significantly based on property size, location, and prevailing circle rates.

stepsToApply: Visit the L&DO e-Dharti portal and register using your mobile number and property details to create an account.
Log into your dashboard and select the desired service (e.g., Conversion to Freehold, Mutation, or Substitution).
Fill in the exhaustive online form linking the previous owner's details with the applicant's details.
Scan and upload all mandatory legal documents, affidavits, and photographs as per the specified checklist.
Pay the calculated processing fees and conversion charges online via the Bharatkosh gateway, and track the status online until the digitally signed certificate is issued.

title: National Generic Document Registration System (NGDRS)
shortDescription: A centralized digital portal for online property valuation, stamp duty calculation, and document registration.
eligibility: Citizens buying, selling, or leasing property in states that have implemented the NGDRS system.

fees.details: Stamp duty and registration fees are calculated dynamically based on state laws, property type, and circle rates.

requiredDocuments: Identity and Address Proofs of Buyer, Seller, and Witnesses
stepsToApply: Visit the NGDRS portal and select your respective State to access the state-specific login page.
Register as a Citizen User and log in to the system. Click on 'Property Valuation' to estimate your stamp duty.
Select 'Document Entry', fill in the details of the property, buyer, seller, and witnesses, and upload the draft deed.
Pay the calculated stamp duty and registration fee online through the integrated state payment gateway (e.g., SHCIL/GRAS).
Book an online appointment slot. Visit the Sub-Registrar's Office (SRO) on the scheduled date with all parties and original documents for biometric capture and final registration.

title: UJALA Scheme (Affordable LEDs)
shortDescription: Distribution of highly subsidized, energy-efficient LED bulbs, tube lights, and energy-efficient fans to domestic consumers.
eligibility: Every domestic household having a metered electricity connection.

fees.details: Subsidized rates: ~₹70 for LED bulbs, ~₹220 for Tube lights, ~₹1110 for Energy efficient fans (prices may vary slightly).

requiredDocuments: Government Photo ID Proof (Aadhaar, Voter ID)
stepsToApply: Visit the official UJALA web portal and navigate to the 'UJALA Dashboard' to find the list of active distribution kiosks in your state/city.
Alternatively, check with your local DISCOM office or designated post offices/CSC centers.
Visit the designated UJALA kiosk physically carrying your latest electricity bill and a valid photo ID.
Present the documents to the kiosk operator, who will verify your consumer number.
Pay the subsidized cash amount and collect your energy-efficient appliances (usually capped at a certain number per household).

ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
title: PAHAL (DBTL) LPG Subsidy Enrollment
shortDescription: Direct Benefit Transfer of LPG subsidy directly into the bank accounts of consumers to prevent leakage.
eligibility: All domestic LPG consumers across the country (except those whose taxable income exceeds ₹10 Lakhs per annum).

fees.details: No enrollment fee.

stepsToApply: Visit the MyLPG.in portal and select your respective gas company (Indane, HP, or BharatGas).
Check your current PAHAL (DBTL) status by entering your 17-digit LPG ID.
If not linked, choose the 'Link Aadhaar' option online. Enter your LPG ID, Aadhaar number, and verify via OTP.
If you prefer offline, download the 'Bank Account - Aadhaar Linkage Form' (Form 1 or Form 2).
Submit Form 1 to your bank to link Aadhaar to your bank account, and Form 2 to your LPG distributor to link Aadhaar to your LPG consumer number.

title: BSNL Bharat Fibre (FTTH) New Connection
shortDescription: Apply online for high-speed fiber-to-the-home (FTTH) broadband and landline connection provided by BSNL.
eligibility: Any citizen residing in an area where BSNL optical fiber infrastructure is available.

fees.details: Security deposit (approx. ₹500), installation charges (approx. ₹250-₹500), plus chosen monthly plan tariff.

stepsToApply: Visit the BSNL 'Book My Fiber' portal or download the BSNL mobile app.
Enter your geographic location using the map interface or PIN code to check FTTH feasibility in your neighborhood.
Select your preferred broadband tariff plan from the available list and fill in your name, mobile number, and installation address.
Submit the request. A lead ID is generated, and a local BSNL representative or franchisee will contact you within 24-48 hours.
The technician will visit your premises for wiring, complete the Aadhaar e-KYC on the spot, collect the installation fee, and activate the high-speed internet connection.

ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
title: Aadhaar Address Update (Online)
shortDescription: Update or change your residential address on your Aadhaar card online without visiting an enrollment center.
eligibility: Any Aadhaar holder whose current mobile number is linked to their Aadhaar card.

fees.details: ₹50 processing fee per update request.

requiredDocuments: Valid Address Proof (Passport, Voter ID, Ration Card, Utility Bill within 3 months, Bank Passbook)
stepsToApply: Visit the 'myAadhaar' portal and log in using your 12-digit Aadhaar number and the OTP sent to your registered mobile number.
Click on the 'Address Update' tab and select 'Update Aadhaar Online'.
Proceed to update address, fill in your new residential address details exactly in English and your local language.
Upload a clear, scanned color copy of your valid Proof of Address document from the accepted list.
Review the demographic data, pay the ₹50 fee via UPI/Netbanking/Card, and download the Service Request Number (SRN) receipt to track your update status.

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
title: RERA Complaint Registration
shortDescription: File a formal complaint against real estate builders, promoters, or agents for project delays or rule violations.
eligibility: Any aggrieved homebuyer, allottee, or real estate agent dealing with a RERA-registered project.

fees.details: Varies by state (Typically ₹1000 for filing a complaint before the Authority; ₹1000-₹5000 for Appellate Tribunal).

stepsToApply: Identify the specific RERA website for your state (e.g., MahaRERA, UP RERA) as operations are state-managed under a central act.
Create a user account as a 'Complainant/Allottee' and log in to the portal.
Click on 'Register Complaint' and fill in the details of the respondent (builder/agent), the registered project details, and the relief sought.
Upload all supporting documents as PDF files and outline the chronology of the dispute.
Pay the state-prescribed complaint filing fee online. A hearing date will be assigned, and you will be notified via SMS/email to attend virtually or physically.

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
title: PM-UDAY (Unauthorized Colonies Regularization)
shortDescription: Conferment of ownership rights to residents of unauthorized colonies in Delhi, allowing them to register properties and take bank loans.
eligibility: Residents holding General Power of Attorney (GPA), Will, Agreement to Sell, or Payment Receipts for properties in 1,731 designated unauthorized colonies in Delhi.

fees.details: Nominal conveyance deed charges based on plot size and circle rate.

stepsToApply: Visit the PM-UDAY portal and click on 'Registration' to create an account using your mobile number and email ID.
Log in and book an appointment with a DDA empanelled GIS agency to conduct a precise geographical survey and generate a geo-tagged map of your property.
Once the GIS agency uploads the polygon map, return to the PM-UDAY portal and fill out the detailed application form.
Upload all chains of property documents (GPA, Agreement to Sell) and electricity bills to establish ownership.
Pay the processing fee. DDA officials will scrutinize the file and conduct a physical inspection before issuing the Conveyance Deed/Authorization Slip for formal registration.

title: PM-WANI (Public Wi-Fi Access)
shortDescription: Framework to elevate broadband penetration by allowing access to public Wi-Fi hotspots provided by Public Data Offices (PDOs).
eligibility: Any citizen with a smartphone seeking internet access.

fees.details: Prices are set by local PDOs, often ranging from ₹5 for 1GB to ₹50 for monthly unlimited access.

requiredDocuments: None (Only a valid mobile number for OTP)
stepsToApply: Visit the PM-WANI central portal to find a list of certified App Providers, or search for 'PM WANI' on your mobile app store.
Download a PM-WANI compliant mobile app (e.g., WANI, Wi-Fi Dabba) on your smartphone.
Open the app, register using your mobile number, and authenticate via OTP.
The app will display nearby PM-WANI Wi-Fi hotspots (PDOs). Walk into the range of a hotspot.
Select a data plan in the app, pay securely via UPI/wallet, and tap 'Connect' to instantly access high-speed internet without needing complex passwords.

ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
title: PM-KUSUM (Solar Pumps for Farmers)
shortDescription: Provides massive subsidies to farmers for installing standalone solar agriculture pumps or solarizing existing grid-connected pumps.
eligibility: Individual farmers, groups of farmers, FPOs, and Water User Associations.

fees.details: Farmers pay only 10% to 40% of the pump cost; the rest is covered by Central and State subsidies and bank loans.

stepsToApply: The scheme is implemented via state nodal agencies (SNAs). Visit your respective state's renewable energy portal or the central PM-KUSUM portal for links.
Register online when the state opens its application window, providing your Aadhaar and land details.
Select the desired capacity of the solar pump (Component B) or grid-connected solarization (Component C) based on your water requirement and land size.
Upload the required land records and identity documents, and submit the initial application fee.
Once shortlisted via the state's selection process, deposit your beneficiary share (usually 10-40%) to the SNA, after which an empanelled vendor will install the solar pump on your farm.

ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
title: Piped Natural Gas (PNG) New Connection
shortDescription: Apply for a safe, uninterrupted domestic Piped Natural Gas (PNG) connection for cooking purposes.
eligibility: Residents living in cities covered under the City Gas Distribution (CGD) network.

fees.details: Refundable security deposit (usually around ₹5,000 to ₹6,000 depending on the CGD entity).

requiredDocuments: Address Proof (Electricity Bill, Property Tax Receipt)
Ownership Proof or NOC from Landlord
stepsToApply: Identify the authorized City Gas Distribution entity operating in your city (e.g., IGL in Delhi, MGL in Mumbai, Adani Gas, GAIL).
Visit the official website of the local CGD provider and click on 'New Domestic PNG Connection'.
Fill in the online application form with your personal details, complete address, and property ownership status.
Upload scanned copies of your ID proof, address proof, and passport-size photograph.
Pay the refundable security deposit online. The company will conduct a feasibility survey, install the meter and pipelines, and convert your existing LPG stove to PNG compatibility.

title: e-Stamping for Rent Agreements
shortDescription: Secure and legally valid online generation of stamp papers for executing rent agreements, affidavits, and leases.
eligibility: Any citizen executing a legal document requiring stamp duty in participating states.

fees.details: Stamp duty amount based on document value/state laws + minor convenience fee.

requiredDocuments: Names and details of First Party (Landlord) and Second Party (Tenant)
stepsToApply: Visit the SHCIL e-Stamping portal and register as a new user.
Log in and select your state from the drop-down menu (note: not all states support online self-printing).
Enter the details of the First Party, Second Party, the article description (e.g., Rent Agreement), and the stamp duty amount required.
Pay the calculated stamp duty online via Net Banking, Debit Card, or UPI.
Download the generated e-Stamp certificate with a unique UIN. Print it on standard A4 paper and print/type your rent agreement text on the subsequent pages.

title: Prepaid Smart Meter Recharge
shortDescription: Track electricity consumption in real-time and recharge your prepaid smart electricity meter online.
eligibility: Electricity consumers where prepaid smart meters have been installed under the Smart Meter National Programme (SMNP).

fees.details: Recharge amount as per consumer usage; no extra portal convenience fees.

stepsToApply: Download the specific Smart Meter mobile app provided by your state DISCOM (e.g., Bihar Bijli Smart NetApp, UP Smart Meter App).
Register using your Consumer Number and registered mobile number.
View your real-time electricity consumption, current balance, and estimated days of supply remaining.
Click on 'Recharge' and enter your desired amount.
Pay securely using UPI or Net Banking. The balance is updated automatically in the meter within 15-30 minutes, preventing power disconnection.

department: Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation
title: GOBARdhan Scheme Registration
shortDescription: Initiative to convert biodegradable waste, especially cattle dung, into biogas (cooking fuel) and organic manure in rural areas.
eligibility: Gram Panchayats, SHGs, FPOs, and rural entrepreneurs setting up biogas or CBG plants.

fees.details: Portal registration is free; provides access to technical support and convergence funding.

stepsToApply: Visit the Unified GOBARdhan portal.
Click on 'Registration' and choose your entity type (Individual, Gram Panchayat, SHG, or Corporate).
Fill out the registration form with your contact details, exact location for the proposed plant, and estimated waste generation capacity.
Submit the form to receive a unique registration number.
Use this registration to apply for financial assistance under Swachh Bharat Mission (Grameen) phase-II or from local nodal agencies to construct your biogas plant.

title: Urja Mitra (Power Outage Tracking)
shortDescription: A unified platform providing real-time information on scheduled and unscheduled power outages across India.
eligibility: Any electricity consumer in India.

fees.details: Free information portal and alert service.

stepsToApply: Visit the Urja Mitra web portal or download the Urja Mitra mobile app.
On the dashboard, you can view the live map of India highlighting current power outages by state and DISCOM.
To receive personalized SMS/email alerts, click on 'Consumer Login/Registration'.
Select your State and DISCOM, and enter your exact Consumer Number.
Verify your mobile number to subscribe. You will now receive automated SMS alerts from your DISCOM prior to scheduled maintenance cuts or during sudden breakdowns.

ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
title: National Consumer Helpline (NCH)
shortDescription: Register grievances regarding faulty real estate projects, overcharging by utilities, or deficient broadband services.
eligibility: Any consumer facing exploitation or deficiency in services by a company or utility provider.

fees.details: Free grievance registration.

stepsToApply: Visit the National Consumer Helpline (INGRAM) portal or download the NCH mobile app.
Click on 'Sign Up' to create a consumer profile using your name, mobile number, and email.
Log in and select 'Register Grievance'. Choose the relevant sector (e.g., Real Estate, Telecom, or Electricity).
Select the company name from the dropdown list, detail your grievance in the text box, and upload supporting documents like bills or agreements.
Submit the complaint. The NCH will assign a docket number and mediate with the company for resolution, usually within 15-45 days.

title: e-Gram Swaraj (Panchayat Tax & Services)
shortDescription: A simplified work-based accounting application enabling rural citizens to pay property/water taxes and view panchayat development plans.
eligibility: Residents of Gram Panchayats where online tax collection is active.

fees.details: Property/Water tax amounts as assessed by the Gram Panchayat; portal usage is free.

stepsToApply: Visit the e-Gram Swaraj portal or download the e-Gram Swaraj mobile application.
Select your State, Zilla Parishad, Block Panchayat, and Gram Panchayat from the dropdown lists.
Navigate to the 'Online Tax Payment' or 'Citizen Services' section (if enabled for your specific state).
Enter your house number or assessment ID to fetch your current property or water tax dues.
Pay the dues securely via the integrated UPI/Netbanking gateway and download the digital receipt for your records.

ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
title: LPG Cylinder Refill Booking (Unified)
shortDescription: Book your LPG cylinder refill online via web, mobile app, SMS, or WhatsApp regardless of your gas agency.
eligibility: Any registered LPG consumer of Indane, BharatGas, or HP Gas.

fees.details: Standard cylinder refill cost as per the current market rate; subsidies are deposited back via DBT.

stepsToApply: Method 1 (WhatsApp): Save your gas company's official verified WhatsApp number. Send 'REFILL' to book instantly from your registered mobile number.
Method 2 (App): Download your provider's app (e.g., IndianOil ONE, Hello BPCL) or a UPI app like GPay/PhonePe.
In the UPI app, go to 'Book a Cylinder', select your provider, and enter your registered mobile number or LPG ID.
The app will fetch the live refill price. Pay the amount securely using UPI PIN.
You will receive a booking reference via SMS, and the filled cylinder will be delivered to your registered address within a few days.

title: Name Change in Electricity Bill
shortDescription: Official process to transfer the electricity connection name upon purchase of property or death of the original owner.
eligibility: New owners or legal heirs of a property with an existing electricity connection.

fees.details: Varies by state DISCOM (typically ₹50 to ₹200 for processing + potential update of security deposit).

requiredDocuments: Registered Sale Deed or Succession Certificate
NOC from the previous owner (if alive)
Aadhaar Card and Passport size photo
stepsToApply: Visit the official website of your specific state's Electricity Distribution Company (e.g., BESCOM, TNEB, UPPCL).
Register/Log in to the consumer portal and navigate to 'Connection Services' or 'Name Change/Transfer of Ownership'.
Enter the existing Consumer Number to fetch the current bill details and verify there are no pending arrears.
Fill out the online application form and upload scanned copies of the Sale Deed, Aadhaar, and NOC/Death Certificate of the previous owner.
Submit the application and pay the processing fee online. The DISCOM will verify the documents and update the name in the subsequent billing cycle.

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
title: Online Property Tax Payment
shortDescription: Pay municipal property taxes online to your respective Municipal Corporation or Urban Local Body seamlessly.
eligibility: Property owners residing in urban areas managed by Municipal Corporations, Municipalities, or Nagar Panchayats.

fees.details: Tax amount is based on the Annual Rental Value or Unit Area Value of the property. Rebates are often given for early online payment.

requiredDocuments: Property Tax Identification Number (PID) / Khata Number
stepsToApply: Identify the official website of your city's Municipal Corporation (e.g., MCGM for Mumbai, NDMC/MCD for Delhi, BBMP for Bengaluru).
Look for the 'Online Services' or 'Citizen Services' tab and click on 'Pay Property Tax'.
Enter your Property Assessment Number, PID, or Khata number to fetch your property records and current outstanding dues.
Verify the owner details, property dimensions, and calculated tax amount on the screen.
Proceed to checkout, make the payment using Net Banking, UPI, or Credit/Debit Card, and immediately download the digitally signed tax receipt for legal record keeping.

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
title: New Water & Sewerage Connection
shortDescription: Apply for a fresh domestic water supply and sewerage line connection from your local municipal authority.
eligibility: Property owners or authorized tenants within municipal limits where municipal pipelines are laid.

fees.details: Application fee + road cutting charges + connection deposit based on pipe diameter and plot size.

requiredDocuments: Property Ownership Document (Sale Deed / Mutation Certificate)
No Dues Certificate for Property Tax
stepsToApply: Visit the official citizen portal of your local Municipal Corporation or State Water Board.
Register a user profile and select 'Apply for New Water and Sewerage Connection' from the dashboard.
Fill out the physical parameter details of your house, required pipe size (e.g., 0.5 inch), and exact geographical location metrics.
Upload scanned copies of your property deed, approved building blueprint, and identity proof vectors.
Submit the application online, pay the initial processing fee, and track the file until a municipal engineer visits your site for technical verification and connection provisioning.

title: New Electricity Connection Application
shortDescription: Apply for a fresh domestic low-tension electricity connection for newly built or occupied properties.
eligibility: Legal owners or lawful occupants of any property requiring electrical connectivity.

fees.details: Fixed processing fee + security deposit + system infrastructure charges based on requested load capacity (kW).

requiredDocuments: Proof of Ownership / Occupancy (Sale Deed, Rent Agreement, or Mutation Receipt)
No Objection Certificate (NOC) if applying in shared or multi-tenant buildings
stepsToApply: Go to the web portal of the Electricity Distribution Company (DISCOM) servicing your area.
Select 'New Connection (Consumer Corner)' and register using your mobile number and email ID.
Fill out Form A online, specifying the requested load (e.g., 2 kW for regular households) and connection type (Domestic).
Upload scanned copies of your property ownership paper, passport-sized photo, and active Aadhaar verification card.
Pay the calculated security deposit and installation fees online; a field assistant will visit to inspect the wiring and install the physical meter.

title: Electricity Load Conversion (Enhancement/Reduction)
shortDescription: Modify the sanctioned electrical load of your existing meter based on changes in your home appliances layout.
eligibility: Registered electricity consumers with up-to-date bill payments.

fees.details: Processing fee + additional security deposit differential if enhancing the load capacity.

requiredDocuments: Identity proof of the registered consumer
Test report from a licensed electrical contractor specifying load details
stepsToApply: Log into your consumer dashboard on your regional DISCOM's official web portal.
Navigate to the 'Load Management / Change of Category' section and select 'Load Conversion'.
Input your current sanctioned load, the newly desired load capability, and detail the heavy appliances causing the request.
Upload a certified digital test report issued by a registered electrical technician confirming safety layouts.
Pay the adjustment charges online; the DISCOM will update the systems and, if necessary, replace the existing service cable layout.

title: Faulty Meter Replacement Request
shortDescription: File an official request to test, repair, or replace an electricity meter that is burnt, stopped, or recording inaccurate readings.
eligibility: Registered consumers experiencing irregular consumption billing patterns or non-functional displays.

fees.details: Free if the meter failed due to technical age; testing fee applies if the consumer challenges accuracy (~₹100-₹500).

requiredDocuments: Copy of the latest irregular electricity bill
Clear photograph showing the physical state of the meter faceplate
stepsToApply: Open your regional DISCOM website or dedicated consumer management mobile application.
Go to the 'Complaints & Requests' tab and choose 'Meter Related Grievances'.
Enter your consumer index details and explicitly choose the defect profile (e.g., Burnt, Defective, Glass Broken).
Upload a high-definition photograph displaying the current reading or the non-responsive screen interface.
Submit the digital form; a testing squad will visit your location to check calibration, and if found faulty, they will replace the unit immediately.

title: Post Office National Savings Scheme (Savings Account)
shortDescription: Open a government-backed retail savings bank account at any Department of Posts branch for banking and utility allocations.
eligibility: Any individual Indian citizen (adults, minors under guardianship, or joint accounts).

fees.details: Minimum initial deposit of ₹500 required to open and sustain the account.

stepsToApply: Visit the official India Post website to download the Account Opening Form (SB-3) or collect it from a nearby Post Office.
Fill out the form completely, selecting the 'Savings Bank Account' option and entering nominee distribution data.
Attach self-attested copies of your Aadhaar card and PAN card to fulfill KYC specifications.
Submit the bundle physically to the Post Office clerk alongside your passport photos and the initial ₹500 minimum cash balance.
Complete basic identity verification checks on the spot to receive your new physical passbook and checkbook manual.

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
title: Online Birth Certificate Registration & Issuance
shortDescription: Apply for the legal registration and digital generation of a birth certificate from municipal records.
eligibility: Parents or legal relatives of a child born within the geographic boundaries of the municipal body.

fees.details: Free registration within 21 days of birth; nominal late fee applicable after the initial window.

requiredDocuments: Institutional Birth Certificate Slip issued by the hospital
stepsToApply: Visit the centralized Civil Registration System (CRS) web engine or your state's e-District municipal workflow hub.
Register a public account profile and select 'Birth Registration Request'.
Populate the digital form fields with the exact date, time, institutional address of birth, and complete parental name details.
Upload the digital copy of the hospital discharge slip tracking number confirming the birth event parameters.
Submit the request; once verified against hospital data records by the health registrar officer, download the digitally signed birth certificate.

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
title: Online Death Certificate Registration
shortDescription: Register a death event online and obtain an officially validated death certificate for legal and estate settlement purposes.
eligibility: Immediate family members or authorized institutional representatives reporting a death event.

fees.details: Zero cost if logged within 21 days; subsequent late filings attract nominal penalty fee schedules.

requiredDocuments: Medical Cause of Death Certificate issued by the hospital or medical examiner
Identity Proof (Aadhaar / Voter card) of the deceased
stepsToApply: Access the official Civil Registration System portal page or your respective state's e-District civic platform.
Log into your system profile and choose 'Death Certificate Request'.
Input demographic parameters of the deceased person alongside exact date, time, and geographic coordinate context of death.
Attach digital copies of the medical death validation certificate and the official cremation center receipt ledger.
Submit the application module online to trigger verification loops by municipal tracking nodes before system generation.

title: Municipal Grievance Redressal (Public Utilities)
shortDescription: File complaints online regarding municipal issues such as open manholes, leaking water mains, or non-functional streetlights.
eligibility: Any citizen residing within the operational territory of the municipal body.

fees.details: Public tracking and registration interfaces carry no usage charges.

requiredDocuments: Photographic evidence of the public utility defect
stepsToApply: Open your designated municipal corporation website (e.g., MCD, BMC) or download their dedicated civic tracking mobile application.
Navigate to the 'Public Grievance Portal / Clean City Complaint' node module option.
Pin the exact geographic location map marker tracking where the breakdown is observed.
Provide a precise textual overview summary of the issue and link your photograph showing the asset defect state.
Submit the entry data card to instantly capture a live tracking token ID for monitoring engineering team responses.

ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
department: Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances
title: CPGRAMS Unified Utility Complaints
shortDescription: National platform to escalate unresolved public utility complaints against central infrastructure bodies (like BSNL, GAIL, NHAI).
eligibility: Any citizen whose complaints remain unaddressed by utility operators after standard processing timeframes.

fees.details: National public grievance processing features carry zero operational costs.

requiredDocuments: Previous complaint tokens history generated by the service provider
Detailed factual case logs summary context documentation
stepsToApply: Open the Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System (CPGRAMS) dashboard portal layout.
Click on 'Register/Login' to authenticate your system profiling metrics using your active mobile number.
Select 'Lodging of Grievance' and match the target node to the specific Central Ministry managing the utility provider.
Enter the historical ticket numbers showing that local resolution pathways failed to address your issue.
Complete form details, upload case files scans, and submit the escalation module to route it directly to public ombudsmen.

ministry: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
title: CPCB Sameer App (Air Quality Utility)
shortDescription: Access hourly National Air Quality Index updates and register localized public environmental health violation alerts.
eligibility: Open to all citizens tracking real-time local air pollution levels.

fees.details: Information accessibility features carry zero transaction bounds.

requiredDocuments: None required to check data metrics; GPS alignment provides local index charts.
stepsToApply: Download the official CPCB 'Sameer' application utility tool via standard mobile system storefronts.
Open the layout map index to view real-time hourly color-coded maps showing national sensor grids.
To report open waste burning or industrial smoke hazards, click the 'Register Complaint' button link.
Capture the event parameters using your device camera module to embed live geo-tag metrics.
Submit the entry profile to dispatch the event directly to localized environmental protection agency teams.

title: BEE Star Label Verification
shortDescription: Verify the energy efficiency credentials and authentic star-rating matrix parameters of home utility appliances.
eligibility: Consumers planning or evaluating the purchase of appliances like ACs, refrigerators, or geysers.

fees.details: Verification and search functions carry zero platform utility costs.

requiredDocuments: Model number and brand identifier indices printed on the appliance energy label
stepsToApply: Visit the BEE Star Label mobile tool or target web portal architecture layouts.
Navigate to the 'Search Appliance Database' module engine menu.
Select the product category (e.g., Room Air Conditioner) and pick the active manufacturer brand descriptor name.
Type the alphanumeric model tracking numbers exactly as displayed on the label layout plate.
Click 'Search' to validate technical metrics against official system records to verify target electricity savings statistics.

title: Groundwater Extraction NOC Application
shortDescription: Mandatory regulatory application to acquire a No Objection Certificate for boring or extracting groundwater for infrastructure projects.
eligibility: Infrastructure projects, commercial establishments, or housing societies setting up deep tubewell layouts.

fees.details: Application fee (~₹1000) + annual water abstraction charges determined by extraction volumes.

requiredDocuments: Rainwater Harvesting system design layout blueprints
stepsToApply: Open the digital portal infrastructure interface managed by the CGWA.
Click on 'Online Application' and build an applicant master profile account system.
Select the correct category matching your setup intent (Industrial, Infrastructure, or Mining).
Populate data fields mapping core water demands alongside structure blueprints of artificial recharge systems.
Pay processing fee matrix schedules online to index the digital file for hydrogeological clearance board review loops.

title: Home Loan Interest Subsidy Status (CLSS)
shortDescription: Track the application progress, disbursement status, and subsidy allocation records under the Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme framework.
eligibility: Home loan applicants who applied for PMAY interest concessions via primary lending institutions.

fees.details: Status query and dashboard monitoring features carry no charge parameters.

requiredDocuments: CLSS Application ID details generated by your lending bank
stepsToApply: Visit the CLSS Awas Portal (CLAP) internet platform node engine.
Locate the dedicated 'PMAY(U) Subsidy Tracker' display window row.
Input your unique system-assigned CLSS application tracking alphanumeric strings.
Authenticate safety profiles by validating the system OTP sent to your Aadhaar-linked phone line numbers.
Monitor your five-stage advancement chart showing processing movement up to final institutional fund release points.

title: Tarang Sanchar (EMF Radiation Mapping)
shortDescription: Check the location profiles of nearby mobile towers and request electromagnetic field radiation testing for peace of mind.
eligibility: Any citizen desiring to verify cell tower radiation safety standard parameters.

fees.details: Browsing the public grid map layout is free; formal custom site validation calls attract a fixed ₹4000 fee.

requiredDocuments: Complete postal residential address details context
stepsToApply: Access the official Tarang Sanchar digital framework interface.
Utilize the system map viewer engine to view tower icons tracking within a specific radius of your layout coordinates.
To query specific certification values, choose the target tower profile indicator node on the display map.
To request localized physical site testing metrics, click 'Request EMF Measurement' and complete the forms.
Submit system transaction fee parameters (~₹4000) online to trigger automated field measurement test dispatches.

ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
title: BIS Care Product Authentication
shortDescription: Verify ISI markings, electronic product registration marks, and gold jewelry hallmark parameters for authenticity.
eligibility: Any individual checking the regulatory quality metrics of household utilities or gold assets.

fees.details: Verification actions run inside zero cost architectures.

requiredDocuments: CM/L license numbers or HUID (Hallmark Unique ID) alphanumerics stamped on the product structure
stepsToApply: Download the 'BIS Care' mobile tracking application via major device operating system marketplaces.
To authenticate domestic utility items, tap 'Verify Licence Details' and enter the stamped CM/L numbers.
To verify precious assets like gold, click 'Verify HUID' tab option structures.
Type the unique 6-digit alphanumeric code present alongside the hallmark stamping scales.
Instantly view laboratory origin profiles, register dates, and corporate validation metrics details.

ministry: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
title: Forest Clearance Application (Housing Infrastructure)
shortDescription: Secure statutory environmental approval for the diversion of forest land sections for essential public utilities or developmental projects.
eligibility: Public institutions, local governing boards, or corporate developers handling permitted infrastructure works.

fees.details: Calculated based on Net Present Value (NPV) metrics of target forest sectors alongside compensatory afforestation fee layers.

requiredDocuments: Geographical shapefiles mapping target perimeter tracking parameters
Detailed project justification layout files summary blueprints
Compensatory Afforestation management project outline metrics documents
stepsToApply: Open the central PARIVESH workflow tracking ecosystem web interface layout.
Create an institutional user account code profile parameters set and select 'Apply for Forest Clearance'.
Complete the exhaustive digital forms (Form A), outlining targeted geographical coordinates and flora impact values.
Upload verified digital shapefile maps showing exact project dimension bounds clearly.
Submit the application file pack to activate linear processing analysis lines via the multi-tiered state forest boards evaluation framework.

title: No Objection Certificate for Building Height (NOCAS)
shortDescription: Mandatory clearance certificate evaluating building height parameters near airfield navigation vectors to ensure aviation safety.
eligibility: Property developers and landowners constructing multi-story buildings within a 56 km radius of active airports.

fees.details: AAI processing and assessment matrices on the NOCAS network carry zero processing fees.

requiredDocuments: Site elevation profile data verified by a registered surveyor
Geographic coordinates structure (WGS-84 system) details mapping corners
stepsToApply: Access the official NOCAS online software platform portal layout.
Complete initial registration to create a secure applicant verification profile.
Select 'New Application' and fill in exact geographic site dimensions using precise coordinates from your survey report.
Upload digital copies of your site plan, coordinate charts, and elevation certificates.
Submit the digital folder parameters to prompt system screening checks that calculate runway airspace layout interactions.

title: Government Accommodation Booking (e-Sampada)
shortDescription: Centralized system managing the allocation, booking, and license fee payment parameters for government residential quarters and holiday homes.
eligibility: Eligible Central Government employees, officials, and designated departments.

fees.details: Monthly license fee (HRA deduction matrix guidelines) adjusted by category allocation rank rules.

requiredDocuments: Official Employee Identity Data records summary parameters
Joining Report details stamped by administrative office hubs
stepsToApply: Open the e-Sampada digital operational platform layout engine portal.
Log into systems by entering your employee verification index credentials or credentials via secure single sign-on tools.
Navigate to the 'Residential Accommodation / General Pool Residential Accommodation' selection pane.
Fill out configuration options detailing priority waitlist indices matching your current grade pay scales.
Submit your selection files digitally to wait for automated system allocation runs executed during periodic cycle openings.

title: NMA Construction Clearance (Heritage Zones)
shortDescription: Secure construction permissions or NOC configurations when building properties located within regulated zones surrounding protected national monuments.
eligibility: Landowners whose property falls inside the 100-300 meter regulated buffer zone around centrally protected monuments.

fees.details: Online application routing through the Smaran framework does not involve charge matrices.

requiredDocuments: Site Plan layout diagrams showing precise distance tracks from monument boundaries
Building elevation structural blueprints indicating absolute height parameters
Local Urban Local Body mapping reference validations
stepsToApply: Access the NMA Smaran online tracking gateway platform interface space.
Complete profile registration steps to establish secure applicant identifier records.
Fill out the comprehensive building proposal profile, entering structural metrics and height limits carefully.
Upload scanned copies of site distance measurements from the monument wall along with structural maps.
Submit your request digitally to trigger inspection visits by local archaeology department oversight teams.

title: Property Mutation Application (Urban Local Bodies)
shortDescription: Officially update transfer records in the municipal asset registration register to change tax obligation definitions.
eligibility: New buyers, beneficiaries of inheritance, or legal gift recipients of properties within urban boundaries.

fees.details: Calculated based on transfer valuation rates or minor baseline fixed administration fee matrix models per state rules.

requiredDocuments: Registered Sale Deed / Gift Deed copy index papers
Death Certificate alongside Succession Matrix paperwork (if handling inheritance chains)
Up-to-date cleared property tax clearance receipt copy documentation
Indemnity Bond executed on legal stamp paper parameters
stepsToApply: Log into the designated state e-District or specific Municipal Corporation civic management online dashboard.
Select 'Property Mutation Service Request' option grids from the primary application panel menu.
Input detailed buyer, seller, historical asset ledger reference indices, and execution date values.
Upload high-quality scanned copies of registered deed transfers, tax certificates, and signed indemnity statements.
Complete online payment tracking loops to route the digital file parameters to the regional revenue inspector box for validation field updates.
